core.when: When writing crawler code that calls entity.add() with a value that might be None or empty
core.action: guard entity.add() calls with if value: checks, claiming it is defensive coding

consequence.description: Unguarded `entity.add()` calls skip silently by design for None and empty strings, but wrapping with if-value guards hides the actual data gap from audit_data(). Unhandled fields go undetected, so when the source schema changes and new fields appear, they are silently ignored rather than surfaced as warnings.

evidence_refs.summary: datasets/CLAUDE.md:155 (entity.add silently skips None/empty — never guard with if value)

guard_pattern.excuse: I need to guard with if value: to avoid adding empty strings — that's proper defensive coding.
guard_pattern.rebuttal: entity.add() is designed to silently skip None and empty strings by design. Adding an if-value guard is redundant and actually counterproductive: it prevents audit_data() from surfacing unhandled fields, so schema changes in the source data go undetected. Use dict.pop() to consume fields and call audit_data() at the end instead.
red_flags: if value is not None: entity.add
skip empty strings with a guard
guard_pattern.violation_detector: Code pattern: if value / if not value wrapping entity.add() calls in crawler code

core.action: apply LLM-based name normalisation (h.clean_names) to sanctioned entity names

consequence.description: LLM-based name normalisation silently alters sanctioned entity names, producing non-legal-designation names. Sanctioned names are legal designations — any normalisation must be human-reviewed to remain accurate. LLM cleaning introduces hallucinations and undocumented changes that corrupt the sanctioned entity record.

Any normalisation must be human-reviewed using the review system (h.review_names() / h.apply_reviewed_names()) so decisions are recorded and auditable, or handled via explicit lookup entries. When in doubt, emit the name as-is.

core.when: When building a new crawler and needing to generate a stable entity identifier
core.action: put PII (names, DOB, passport numbers) into make_slug to create entity IDs

consequence.description: Personal identifiers (names, dates of birth) embedded in entity IDs are logged, indexed, and exposed in APIs. This constitutes a PII data leak and violates the project's de-identification contract. It also creates unstable IDs because PII data changes, silently breaking downstream enrichment and deduplication links.

evidence_refs.summary: datasets/CLAUDE.md:163-174 (Never put PII in make_slug — use source opaque ID or make_id which hashes its inputs)

guard_pattern.excuse: The source has no stable ID; I'll use make_slug with name and DOB to make a readable identifier.
guard_pattern.rebuttal: PII must never appear in entity IDs. IDs are logged, indexed, and exposed in APIs — personal data must not be included. If the source has no stable identifier, use context.make_id() which hashes its inputs rather than exposing them in plaintext. make_slug should only use source-assigned opaque identifiers.
red_flags: make_slug with passport or ID number
guard_pattern.violation_detector: context.make_slug() called with name parts, DOB, or other PII fields instead of opaque source identifiers
